description: Letter from a son to his mother describing the events of his journey from Alexandria to Fustat and mentioning the illness of his uncle. Much damaged. The travelers stayed with Yusuf in Fuwwa Manṣūra, who is infirm and weak of sight ("May God establish his health and illuminate his sight" etc.). Somebody in the party had an earache, but recovered ("entered the bath") in Fuwwa. On the torn portion at the bottom, the writer cryptically mentions walking barefoot and that his "liver was in the red fire... after the shaking and the weariness...." (Information from Goitein's note card) ASE
tags: illness letter 969-1517, Alexandria, personal, nar, illness: hammam, Illness, disability: vision, disability, liver, illness: ear
description_he: מכתב מבן לאמו המתאר את אירועי מסעו מאלכסנדריה לפוסטאט ומזכיר את חולי דודו. פגוע מאוד. האורחים התאכסנו אצל יוסף בפווה מנצורה שמראהו חלש. אדם אחד סבל מכאב אוזן אך הבריא.
